'''Waqa Blake''' (born 26 October 1994) is a Fijian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|wg}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] and {{rlnt|Fiji}} at international level.

He previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] and the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[National Rugby League]] and [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]].

==Background==
Blake was born in [[Lautoka|Lautoka, Fiji]], Blake moved to [[Sydney|Sydney, New South Wales, Australia]] as a 9-year-old.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/waqa-blake-a-future-state-of-origin-star-says-brad-fittler/news-story/a43262931fdcd54adfecae2ed51ed87c |title=Waqa Blake a future State of Origin star, says Brad Fittler |publisher=Fox Sports |date= |access-date=2 January 2023 |archive-date=1 January 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230101232706/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/waqa-blake-a-future-state-of-origin-star-says-brad-fittler/news-story/a43262931fdcd54adfecae2ed51ed87c |url-status=live }}</ref> Initially a rugby union player, Blake got introduced to rugby league by accident as his mother enrolled him in the wrong code.

He played his junior football for the Brighton Seagulls. Blake then moved to [[Perth]] and played for [[Joondalup Giants]] in the [[Western Australian Rugby League|Perth Rugby League]] competition before making the [[West Coast Pirates|Perth Pirates]] [[S. G. Ball Cup]] representative team.<ref name="fittler">{{cite news|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/waqa-blake-a-future-state-of-origin-star-says-brad-fittler/story-fni3gfvk-1227322161680|archive-url=https://archive.today/20150430130733/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/waqa-blake-a-future-state-of-origin-star-says-brad-fittler/story-fni3gfvk-1227322161680|url-status=dead|archive-date=30 April 2015|title=Waqa Blake a Future State of Origin Star|author=Walshaw, Nick|newspaper=Daily Telegraph|date=26 April 2015|access-date=30 April 2015}}</ref> At the end of 2012, after being spotted playing for the Pirates, Blake was signed by the Penrith Panthers.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/penrith-panthers/panthers-rookie-waqa-blake-played-rugby-league-by-accident-20150410-1mi46x.html|title=Panther Rookie Waqa Blake played Rugby League by Accident|author=Chammas, Michael|newspaper=Sydney Morning Herald|date=10 April 2015|access-date=30 April 2015|archive-date=15 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150515091905/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/penrith-panthers/panthers-rookie-waqa-blake-played-rugby-league-by-accident-20150410-1mi46x.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===2013===
Blake plays for a [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/b/waqa-blake/|title=Waqa Blake|work=NYC DATABASE|access-date=2015-12-25|archive-date=19 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150419200603/http://nycdatabase.org/players/b/waqa-blake/|url-status=usurped}}</ref> Blake played in the Penrith club's [[2013 NRL Under-20s season results#Grand final|2013 NYC Grand Final]] against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] at centre, scoring a try in Penrith's 42-30 win.<ref>{{cite news| work=Daily Telegraph| title=Penrith Panthers seal thrilling 42-30 Holden Cup victory over New Zealand Warriors| author=Ben Horne| url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/penrith-panthers-seal-thrilling-42-30-holden-cup-victory-over-new-zealand-warriors/story-fni3gfvk-1226733775052| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131108001823/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/penrith-panthers-seal-thrilling-42-30-holden-cup-victory-over-new-zealand-warriors/story-fni3gfvk-1226733775052| url-status=dead| archive-date=8 November 2013| access-date=16 July 2015}}</ref>
[[File:waqablake.jpg|thumb|right|Blake playing for the Panthers]]

===2014===
In May 2014, Blake was named on the interchange bench for the Fiji international team for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations#Samoa v. Fiji|Pacific Test]] against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] but was later replaced by [[Petero Civoniceva]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://m.nrl.com/nrl-big-guns-for-samoa-fiji-teams/tabid/10874/newsid/77980/default.aspx|title=NRL big guns for Samoa, Fiji teams|work=NRL.com|access-date=2015-12-25|archive-date=16 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150716162216/http://m.nrl.com/nrl-big-guns-for-samoa-fiji-teams/tabid/10874/newsid/77980/default.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| work=Courier Mail| title=Samoa ruin Fijian prop Petero Civoniceva's shock comeback to rugby league| first=Peter| last=Badel| url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/samoa-ruin-fijian-prop-petero-civonicevas-shock-comeback-to-rugby-league/story-fniabjcr-1226904486649| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140506135859/http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/samoa-ruin-fijian-prop-petero-civonicevas-shock-comeback-to-rugby-league/story-fniabjcr-1226904486649| url-status=dead| archive-date=6 May 2014| access-date=18 July 2015| date=3 May 2014}}</ref>

In the second half of the 2014 season, Blake moved on to Penrith's  [[New South Wales Cup]] team, later earning selection in the [[New South Wales Residents rugby league team|New South Wales Residents]] team at centre in the 24-16 loss against [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/vb-nsw-cup-representative-side-named/tabid/10874/newsid/79851/default.aspx|title=VB NSW Cup Representative side named|work=NRL.com|date=July 2014|access-date=2015-12-25|archive-date=16 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150716112019/http://www.nrl.com/vb-nsw-cup-representative-side-named/tabid/10874/newsid/79851/default.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/queensland-residents-scrape-home/tabid/10874/newsid/80096/default.aspx|title=Queensland Residents scrape home|work=NRL.com|date=9 July 2014|access-date=2015-12-25|archive-date=16 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150716104546/http://www.nrl.com/queensland-residents-scrape-home/tabid/10874/newsid/80096/default.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 28 September 2014, Blake played at centre in Penrith's [[2014 New South Wales Cup#Grand Final|2014 New South Wales Cup Grand Final]] against the [[Newcastle Knights]] and scored a try in the club's 48-12 win.<ref>{{cite news| work= Daily Telegraph| title=Tonga smash Samoa 36-4| author=Adrian Warren| url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/tonga-smash-samoa-36-4/story-e6frexnr-1226625137385?nk=be0dfabf75e2ad5c52311115b306a4d2|access-date=7 July 2015|date=20 July 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news| work=Daily Telegraph| title=Penrith Panthers Take VB NSW Cup Title With Win Over Newcastle Knights| url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/penrith-panthers-take-vb-nsw-cup-title-with-win-over-newcastle-knights/story-fni3gfvk-1227073149668| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140929130856/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/penrith-panthers-take-vb-nsw-cup-title-with-win-over-newcastle-knights/story-fni3gfvk-1227073149668| url-status=dead| archive-date=29 September 2014| access-date=16 July 2015}}</ref> On 5 October 2014, Blake scored a try in the Penrith club's 32-28 defeat by the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]] in the [[2014 New South Wales Cup#2014 NRL State Championship match|2014 NRL State Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/northern-pride-stun-penrith-panthers-in-nrls-state-championship-20141005-10qjm2.html|title=Northern Pride stun Penrith Panthers in NRL's State Championship|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=5 October 2014|access-date=6 April 2015|archive-date=5 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150705202451/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/northern-pride-stun-penrith-panthers-in-nrls-state-championship-20141005-10qjm2.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2015===
On 26 February 2015, Blake extended his contract with Penrith from the end of 2016 to the end of 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/panthers-re-sign-peachey-blake/|title=PANTHERS RE-SIGN PEACHEY AND BLAKE|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=16 July 2015|archive-date=16 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150716224145/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/panthers-re-sign-peachey-blake/|url-status=live}}</ref> In Round 5 of the [[2015 NRL season]], Blake made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Penrith Panthers against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] at centre in Penrith's 30-10 loss at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news| work= Courier Mail| title=North Queensland Cowboys defeat Penrith Panthers 30-10 inspired by Johnathan Thurston| author=Chris Garry| url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/north-queensland-cowboys-defeat-penrith-panthers-30-10-inspired-by-johnathan-thurston/story-fniabn55-1227293488911}}</ref> In Blake's next match in Round 6 against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], Blake scored his first [[National Rugby League|NRL]] career try in the Penrith club's 22-12 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/penrith-panthers-beat-manly-sea-eagles-in-injuryravaged-clash-20150411-1mj3qr.html|title=Penrith Panthers beat Manly Sea Eagles in injury-ravaged clash|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=11 April 2015|access-date=16 July 2015|archive-date=18 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150718074701/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/penrith-panthers-beat-manly-sea-eagles-in-injuryravaged-clash-20150411-1mj3qr.html|url-status=live}}</ref> On 3 May 2015, Blake played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] against [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country]], playing at centre in City's 34-22 loss.<ref name="fittler" /><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|work=NRL.com|date=3 May 2015|access-date=2015-12-25|archive-date=5 May 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150505202335/http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/city-country-nsw-origin-coach-laurie-daley-praises-matt-moylan-josh-jackson/story-fni3fqyo-1227333110812|title=City Country Origin Coach Laurie Daley Praises Matt Moylan, Josh Jackson|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-12-25}}{{dead link|date=October 2023}}</ref> He finished off his debut year in the NRL having played in 18 matches and scoring 5 tries for the Panthers.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/OC0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMTQ5MS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-29|access-date=2015-12-25|archive-date=26 August 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230826051905/https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/OC0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMTQ5MS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2016===
In February 2016, Blake was named in Penrith's [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url = http://www.nrl.com/2016-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-official-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/91563/default.aspx|title = 2016 Downer NRL Auckland Nines Official Squads|date = 3 February 2016|access-date = 25 March 2017|archive-date = 13 January 2018|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20180113202950/https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/02/03/2016-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-official-squads/|url-status = live}}</ref> After [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] international representatives and usual backline starters [[Peta Hiku]] and [[Dean Whare]] suffered season ending knee injuries, Blake made the right centre position of his own, having some blockbusting performances in the late rounds of the season during the Panthers charge into the finals, a great transformation after he explained that he returned to pre-season training 12 kilograms overweight and coach [[Anthony Griffin (rugby league)|Anthony Griffin]] mentioning to him that "if you want to play in the outside backs, you need to trim down, otherwise you'll be playing in the forwards".<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/panthers-ace-waqa-blake-only-has-an-appetite-for-terrorising-nrl-rivals/news-story/314288086fc7aad3fc29ffecaf9389e6 |title=‘I looked like a Swiss ball. I was round’ |publisher=Daily Telegraph |access-date=25 March 2017|url-access=subscription}}</ref> Blake enjoyed his [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing with playing in 21 matches and scoring 8 tries for the Penrith club. On 14 November 2016, Blake extended his contract with Penrith to the end of the [[2019 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/penrith-panthers-impressive-retention-drive-continues-with-waqa-blake/news-story/a7869c6619293dfdd245d8433954afab|title=Penrith Panthers' impressive retention drive continues with Waqa Blake|date=14 November 2016|access-date=4 April 2018|archive-date=14 November 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161114173850/http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/penrith-panthers-impressive-retention-drive-continues-with-waqa-blake/news-story/a7869c6619293dfdd245d8433954afab|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2017===
In February 2017, Blake was named in Penrith's runners-up [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad where he was named in the Team of the Tournament.<ref>{{Cite web|url = http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/auckland-nines/auckland-nines-2017-teens-and-tyros-headline-team-of-the-tournament/news-story/ed6fa4870af397b9098421b5d116e771|title = Auckland Nines 2017: Teens and tyros headline team of the tournament|date = 5 February 2017|access-date = 25 March 2017|archive-date = 6 May 2017|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20170506173407/http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/auckland-nines/auckland-nines-2017-teens-and-tyros-headline-team-of-the-tournament/news-story/ed6fa4870af397b9098421b5d116e771|url-status = live}}</ref> After the Penrith club's Round 5 clash against the [[Melbourne Storm]], where they lost 28-6 at [[AAMI Park]], Blake alongside Panthers captain [[Matt Moylan]] and [[Peta Hiku]] were dropped to Penrith's [[NSW Cup]] team for 1 match after they breached the booze curfew by being out in the [[Melbourne, Victoria|Melbourne]] nightlife into the early hours of the next morning.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/panthers-captain-matt-moylan-peta-hiku-and-waqa-blake-dropped-for-team-protocol-breach/news-story/1391986c013ff978eed668f520bffdc2 |title=Panthers captain Matt Moylan, Peta Hiku and Waqa Blake dropped for ‘team protocol breach’ |publisher=Daily Telegraph |date=4 April 2017 |access-date=2 January 2023 |archive-date=25 October 2023 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20231025100047/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/panthers-captain-matt-moylan-peta-hiku-and-waqa-blake-dropped-for-team-protocol-breach/news-story/1391986c013ff978eed668f520bffdc2 |url-status=live }}</ref> On 6 May 2017, Blake made his international debut for [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]], playing at centre in the 26-24 loss at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/05/04/tonga-v-fiji-preview/|title=Tonga v Fiji preview|date=4 May 2017|access-date=5 April 2018|archive-date=5 April 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180405153056/https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/05/04/tonga-v-fiji-preview/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/unlikely-hero-leilani-latu-inspires-12man-tonga-to-a-thrilling-victory-over-fiji/news-story/bdd8f6d8c4d3f8530cf994b465d5609f|title=Unlikely hero inspires 12-man Tonga to victory|date=6 May 2017|access-date=5 April 2018|archive-date=25 October 2023|archive-url=https://archive.today/20231025101316/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/unlikely-hero-leilani-latu-inspires-12man-tonga-to-a-thrilling-victory-over-fiji/news-story/bdd8f6d8c4d3f8530cf994b465d5609f|url-status=live}}</ref> In Round 26 against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], Blake suffered a season-ending shoulder injury during the Panthers 28-12 loss at [[Brookvale Oval]], unfortunately resulting him of missing the finals series and representing Fiji for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url = https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/09/08/penriths-blake-out-of-manly-clash/|title = Penrith's Blake out of Manly clash|date = 7 September 2017|access-date = 5 April 2018|archive-date = 5 April 2018|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20180405153423/https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/09/08/penriths-blake-out-of-manly-clash/|url-status = live}}</ref> Blake finished the [[2017 NRL season]] with him playing in 23 matches and scoring 7 tries for the Panthers.

===2018===
In Round 1 against cross-city rivals, the [[Parramatta Eels]], Blake started the 2018 season with a bang, scoring two tries for Penrith as they came back from a 14-0 deficit to achieve a 24-14 victory at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nathan-cleary-leads-penrith-panthers-to-2414-win-over-parramatta-eels/news-story/e35ba9d38ed5d4531fb781b2a0a318b6 |title=What troubles? Panthers put off-field woes behind them |publisher=Daily Telegraph |access-date=6 June 2018|url-access=subscription}}</ref> As Blake showed great form in the early rounds, unfortunately in Round 5 against the [[Parramatta Eels]], who the Penrith clubplayed against a month earlier, Blake suffered a high-grade syndesmosis injury in his right ankle in late in second-half as the Panthers walked away with a close 12-6 win at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/waqa-blake-adds-to-panthers-injury-woes-20180409-p4z8li.html|title=Waqa Blake adds to Panthers' injury woes|date=9 April 2018|access-date=6 June 2018|archive-date=13 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180613222911/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/waqa-blake-adds-to-panthers-injury-woes-20180409-p4z8li.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Blake was set to miss four rounds but unfortunately was ruled out for longer. Blake finally made his return for Penrith in Round 17 against the [[New Zealand Warriors]], scoring a try in the 36-4 upset win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news|url = https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/new-zealand-were-completely-outplayed-by-a-rampant-penrith/news-story/beb6c78d5e9719b079676f6a381545a7|title = 'Very disappointing': Warriors embarrassed|newspaper = News.com.au|date = 6 July 2018|access-date = 8 September 2018|archive-date = 9 September 2018|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20180909000236/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/new-zealand-were-completely-outplayed-by-a-rampant-penrith/news-story/beb6c78d5e9719b079676f6a381545a7|url-status = live}}</ref> Blake finished the [[2018 NRL season]] with him being Penrith's highest tryscorer with 12 tries in 15 matches.

===2019===
Blake started the [[2019 NRL season]] as one of Penrith's first choice centres.  Blake would play the first 10 games of the season for Penrith as the club struggled on the field winning only 2 of their first 9 matches which left the side last on the table.  Blake's final game for Penrith was in Round 11 against Parramatta at the new [[Western Sydney Stadium]] which Penrith won 16-10.  The following week, it was revealed that Blake would be ruled out for 10 weeks with a knee injury.  On 26 June, it was announced that Blake was released by Penrith and had signed a four-and-a-half-year deal with Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/eels-snatch-panthers-star-in-midseason-signing-frenzy/news-story/ee17e69cec14f0838f6b2c814ea297c3/|title=Eels snatch Panthers star in mid-season signing frenzy|website=Fox Sports|date=26 June 2019|access-date=16 July 2019|archive-date=16 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210916194253/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/eels-snatch-panthers-star-in-midseason-signing-frenzy/news-story/ee17e69cec14f0838f6b2c814ea297c3|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2019/05/25/westfund-injury-update-waqa-blake/|title=Westfund Injury Update: Waqa Blake|website=Penrith Panthers|date=25 May 2019|access-date=16 July 2019|archive-date=28 May 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190528064926/https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2019/05/25/westfund-injury-update-waqa-blake/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-23/nrl-penrith-panthers-beat-parramatta-eels-16-10/11137726/|title=Penrith Panthers beat Parramatta Eels 16-10 to end NRL losing run|website=ABC|date=23 May 2019|access-date=16 July 2019|archive-date=22 July 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190722051834/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-23/nrl-penrith-panthers-beat-parramatta-eels-16-10/11137726|url-status=live}}</ref>

Blake made his debut for Parramatta against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] which ended in a 24–22 victory at the new [[Western Sydney Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-parramatta-eels-edge-new-zealand-warriors-2422-in-controversial-stunner/news-story/61d6dad127cdadecbd6eba1d8a3a251b/|title=Parramatta Eels edge New Zealand Warriors 24-22 in controversial stunner|website=News.com.au|date=27 July 2019|access-date=13 August 2019|archive-date=13 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190813135438/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-parramatta-eels-edge-new-zealand-warriors-2422-in-controversial-stunner/news-story/61d6dad127cdadecbd6eba1d8a3a251b/|url-status=live}}</ref>

In Round 22 against the Gold Coast, Blake scored his first and second tries for Parramatta in a 36–12 victory at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/moses-guides-eels-past-sorry-titans-in-nrl-c-403444/|title=Moses guides Eels past sorry Titans in NRL|website=7 News|date=16 August 2019|access-date=16 August 2019|archive-date=16 August 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190816104234/https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/moses-guides-eels-past-sorry-titans-in-nrl-c-403444|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2020===
In round 5 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Blake scored a try and had one try assist as Parramatta defeated his former club Penrith 16-10.  The result saw Parramatta win their first five games of the year, the club's best start to a season since 1986.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-06-12/nrl-warriors-cowboys-parramatta-penrith/12350454/|title=Friday Night Results|website=www.abc.net|date=12 June 2020|access-date=13 June 2020|archive-date=25 October 2023|archive-url=https://archive.today/20231025103831/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-06-12/nrl-warriors-cowboys-parramatta-penrith/12350454|url-status=live}}</ref>

Blake played a total of 22 games for Parramatta in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club finished third but were eliminated in straight sets from the finals.  Blake came under intense criticism in the second half of the season after numerous poor defensive displays.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/parramatta-eels-farewell-11-players-in-mass-exodus-following-disappointing-finals-exit/1txqr55h228vq1wxk66ecwybmr/|title=Parramatta Eels farewell 11 players in mass exodus following disappointing finals exit|website=www.sportingnews.com|access-date=14 October 2020|archive-date=12 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201012223938/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/parramatta-eels-farewell-11-players-in-mass-exodus-following-disappointing-finals-exit/1txqr55h228vq1wxk66ecwybmr|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/i-know-what-i-m-capable-of-waqa-blake-ignores-tsunami-of-criticism-20201006-p562id.html/|title=I know what I am capable of|website=wwwsmh.com.au|date=6 October 2020|access-date=14 October 2020|archive-date=7 October 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201007080302/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/i-know-what-i-m-capable-of-waqa-blake-ignores-tsunami-of-criticism-20201006-p562id.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 1 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Blake was injured in Parramatta's 24-16 victory over Brisbane and ruled out for an indefinite period.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-03-12/brisbane-broncos-lose-to-parramatta-eels-xavier-coates-injured/13242554/|title=Brisbane Broncos' NRL losing streak continues as Parramatta Eels score comeback 24-16 win; Newcastle Knights beat Bulldogs|website=www.abc.net.au|date=12 March 2021|access-date=4 May 2021|archive-date=25 October 2023|archive-url=https://archive.today/20231025104154/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-03-12/brisbane-broncos-lose-to-parramatta-eels-xavier-coates-injured/13242554|url-status=live}}</ref>

Blake returned to the Parramatta starting side for their round 10 victory over the [[New Zealand Warriors]].  The following week, Blake was badly exposed in defence during Parramatta's 28-6 loss against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]].  Blake missed six tackles throughout the game, gave away four penalties and was sin binned in the 72nd minute.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/23/right-edge-review-after-blake-badly-exposed-by-manly/|title=Right-edge review after Blake badly exposed by Manly|website=www.nrl.com|date=23 May 2021|access-date=23 May 2021|archive-date=23 May 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210523110859/https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/23/right-edge-review-after-blake-badly-exposed-by-manly/|url-status=live}}</ref>

Blake made a total of 17 appearances for Parramatta in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the club's two finals matches against [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] and [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-12/nrl-parramatta-eels-defeat-newcastle-knights/100436668/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat Newcastle Knights 28-20 in NRL elimination final|newspaper=ABC News|date=12 September 2021|access-date=1 October 2021|archive-date=21 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210921083925/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-12/nrl-parramatta-eels-defeat-newcastle-knights/100436668|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-21/penrith-panthers-nrl-breach-notice-trainer-semi-final-parramatta/100479588/|title=Penrith Panthers handed NRL breach notice, fined $25,000 after incident in Parramatta semi-final|newspaper=ABC News|date=21 September 2021|access-date=1 October 2021|archive-date=28 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210928074418/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-21/penrith-panthers-nrl-breach-notice-trainer-semi-final-parramatta/100479588|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 4 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Blake scored two tries for Parramatta in a 48–14 victory over [[St. George Illawarra|St. George]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-parramatta-eels-vs-st-george-illawarra-dragons-live-updates-supercoach-scores-video-mitchell-moses-ben-hunt/news-story/a541656e52d581102e601e989dc31b2b/|title=The 'sharp knife' set to trigger Eels title surge as Dragons' big signing flops: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=3 April 2022|access-date=25 April 2022|archive-date=25 April 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220425134052/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-parramatta-eels-vs-st-george-illawarra-dragons-live-updates-supercoach-scores-video-mitchell-moses-ben-hunt/news-story/a541656e52d581102e601e989dc31b2b/|url-status=live}}</ref>
In round 5 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Blake was taken from the field during the club's 26-20 victory over the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]].  It was later announced that Blake would be ruled out from playing indefinitely with a knee injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2022/04/11/injury-update-waqa-blake/|title=Injury Update: Waqa Blake|website=www.parraeels.com.au|date=11 April 2022|access-date=11 April 2022|archive-date=11 April 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220411060025/https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2022/04/11/injury-update-waqa-blake/|url-status=live}}</ref>
In round 18, Blake scored two tries for Parramatta in a 28–18 victory over the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-parramatta-eels-vs-warriors-live-stream-live-blog-supercoach-scores-videos-mitchell-moses-shaun-johnson/news-story/e5bbf513a671eda26fda53d00a6f08a3/|title=The 'key cog' that puts clock on Eels' title window; Benji pinpoints big Warriors 'shift': 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|access-date=15 July 2022|archive-date=15 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220715231715/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-parramatta-eels-vs-warriors-live-stream-live-blog-supercoach-scores-videos-mitchell-moses-shaun-johnson/news-story/e5bbf513a671eda26fda53d00a6f08a3/|url-status=live}}</ref>
In round 23, Blake scored two tries for Parramatta in a 42–6 victory over arch-rivals [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-08-20/nrl-round-23-one-sided-games-roosters-sharks-eels/101352520/|title=Roosters, Eels and Sharks add pre-finals drubbings to one-sided round of NRL|website=www.abc.net.au|access-date=20 August 2022|archive-date=20 August 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220820134924/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-08-20/nrl-round-23-one-sided-games-roosters-sharks-eels/101352520|url-status=live}}</ref>
Blake played a total of 22 games for Parramatta throughout the year, including all three of the club's finals matches and the [[2022 NRL Grand Final]] loss to [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2022/oct/02/nrl-grand-final-2022-live-penrith-panthers-vs-parramatta-eels-scores-winners-win-team-news-lists-kickoff-start-time-game-score-updates-teams|title=NRL Grand Final as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com|access-date=3 October 2022|archive-date=3 October 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221003180846/https://amp.theguardian.com/sport/live/2022/oct/02/nrl-grand-final-2022-live-penrith-panthers-vs-parramatta-eels-scores-winners-win-team-news-lists-kickoff-start-time-game-score-updates-teams|url-status=live}}</ref>

===2023===
After a number of indifferent performances to start the year, Blake was demoted to the [[NSW Cup]] for the club's round 6 match against the [[Wests Tigers]]. Blake was replaced in the centre position by [[Sean Russell (rugby league)|Sean Russell]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-wests-tigers-vs-parramatta-eels-live-stream-live-blog-supercoach-scores-videos-mitchell-moses-luke-brooks/news-story/a20fc8f9f5a9004b4fd4cf025f8ce0d9/|title=Moses proves his value and shows Tigers what they've been missing amid Doueihi disaster - 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|access-date=17 April 2023|archive-date=17 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230417035849/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-wests-tigers-vs-parramatta-eels-live-stream-live-blog-supercoach-scores-videos-mitchell-moses-luke-brooks/news-story/a20fc8f9f5a9004b4fd4cf025f8ce0d9|url-status=live}}</ref>
Blake spent the next three months in reserve grade before being recalled to the first grade team due to the suspension of [[Maika Sivo]]. Blake made his long awaited return in the club's  24-16 loss against North Queensland.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-premiership/2023/round-21/cowboys-v-eels/|title=North Queensland 24 Parramatta 16|website=www.nrl.com|access-date=28 July 2023|archive-date=28 July 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230728112735/https://www.nrl.com/draw/nrl-premiership/2023/round-21/cowboys-v-eels/|url-status=live}}</ref>
Following a few indifferent performances, Blake was left out of the Parramatta side for their round 23 match against [[St. George Illawarra]] as he was replaced by [[Isaac Lumelume]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/08/01/nrl-team-lists-round-23/|title=NRL Team Lists Round 23|website=www.nrl.com|access-date=1 August 2023|archive-date=1 August 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230801094809/https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/08/01/nrl-team-lists-round-23/|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 31 October, Blake was released by Parramatta after not being offered a new contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-offseason-central-parramatta-eels-departing-players-waqa-blake-josh-hodgson-jack-murchie-samuel-loizou-andrew-davey/news-story/cc3201cf8c82068b3c5bf3311a52df46/|title=Grand Final flyer let go as Eels farewell FIVE departing players — Off-season Central|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2024===
On 11 January 2024, it was reported that Blake had signed for [[St Helens R.F.C.]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Jan 2024|title=St Helens confirm Waqa Blake signing as Fiji star takes the vacant No. 3 jersey: ‘I can’t wait’|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-confirm-waqa-blake-signing-as-fiji-star-takes-the-vacant-no-3-jersey-i-cant-wait}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Blake made his club debut for St Helens in their 40-4 victory over the [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304742|title=Super League: St Helens 40-4 London Broncos - Saints thrash promoted side|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Following an indifferent start to his time at St Helens, Blake was demoted to the clubs reserves team ahead of their Good Friday match against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/24233540.waqa-blake-dropped-saints-reserves-weekends-match/|title=Waqa Blake dropped to Saints Reserves for weekend's match|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 11, Blake scored a [[hat-trick]] in St Helens 60-6 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cw590561l69o|title=St Helens romp to 10-try victory at Castleford|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 14, Blake scored two tries for St Helens in their 52-6 victory over London at the [[Twickenham Stoop]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqll4833nvqo|title=Nine-try Saints hammer Broncos to return to top|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 2 October, Blake was released by St Helens after not being offered a new contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/st-helens-confirm-seven-player-exits-with-one-major-name-omitted-from-list-97627/|title=St Helens confirm seven player exits with one major name omitted from list|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>
On 15 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Nov 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Waqa Blake’s first words after joining Championship heavyweights following St Helens departure|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-st-helens-waqa-blake-joins-championship-bradford-bulls}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=2024-11-15 |title=Ex-St Helens player Waqa Blake finds surprise new club |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-st-helens-player-waqa-blake-finds-surprise-new-club/ |access-date=2024-11-16 |website=Total Rugby League |language=en-US}}</ref>

===2025=== 
He is part of the bulls promotion &  return to [[English Super League]] for the first time in 10 years to start in 2026,

==International==
In October, Blake was selected by Fiji for the [[2023 Pacific Rugby League Championships]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/videos/nrl/klein-praised-for-stunning-fiji-try/clo13h7hp00ce0jnzz7qka6xn/|title=Klein praised for stunning Fiji try|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>
